title: Ceropegia sinaica
taxonomy: Ceropegia sinaica is classified within the family Apocynaceae. This family includes approximately 5,000 species of plants, commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. Ceropegia sinaica is further classified within the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. The genus Ceropegia itself belongs to the tribe Periplocoideae within the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. The exact classification of Ceropegia sinaica might be subject to ongoing research and updates in plant taxonomy.

intro: Ceropegia sinaica, also known as Devil's Claw, is a perennial plant species native to Africa, characterized by its unique claw-like shape and adaptogenic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate various health issues, including pain, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and enhancing immune function. The bioactive constituents of Ceropegia sinaica include harpagoside and other iridoid glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Devil's Claw include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, fibromyalgia, and other musculoskeletal disorders.
